message: I finished the Tamagushi offering, a ritual offering performed during the overnight vigil in the Shinto tradition.
Then I noticed for the first time that Hasaki wasn't there.

name: Tomosane
message: "Hey, Yuki-nee?"

name: Yuki
message: "Hm? What's up?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Have you seen Hasaki?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? Wasn't she with you?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm pretty sure she was here, but after I offered the Tamagushi, she was gone."

name: Yuki
message: "Maybe she's in the bathroom?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I already checked the bathroom in the house, but she wasn't there."

name: Yuki
message: "Where the heck is she?"
Yuki-nee quickly ran off to search around the house.
I searched the area around the house too, looking in every place I could think of.

name: Yuki
message: "Find her?"

name: Tomosane
message: "No, I didn't. I asked some other people too."

name: Minakami
message: "Tomosane, did you say Hasaki-chan is gone?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Master! Um, she's not in the house. I even checked the cupboards and drawers."

name: Yuki
message: "We searched around the house too, but she's not around here."

name: Minakami
message: "Why would she run off at this time?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't know. I think she was here before I offered the Tamagushi, but then when I turned around she was gone."

name: Yuki
message: "Anyway, can you get some of the students to help us look for her?"

name: Minakami
message: "Sure, of course. I'll go ask everyone that isn't busy."

name: Yuki
message: "Let's go, Tomosane. We should go look by the water."

name: Tomosane
message: "The water?"

name: Yuki
message: "That's the most dangerous place. We wouldn't want her to hurt herself!"

name: Minakami
message: "I'll go look over by the beach."

name: Yuki
message: "At her age, I don't think she could really get all the way out to the beach, so we don't have to worry about that."

name: Yuki
message: "All right, Tomosane and I will go look by the irrigation ditches."

name: Minakami
message: "I'll leave it up to you."

name: Yuki
message: "Haa, haa, she's not over by the ditch... I'm pretty sure."

name: Yuki
message: "There was a pond out there, right?"

name: Yuki
message: "What's wrong, Tomosane?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Oh, it's just... I think Hasaki said something weird earlier."

name: Yuki
message: "Something weird?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Uh, there's a road behind the school, right?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, there is."

name: Tomosane
message: "She asked what was down that road."

name: Yuki
message: "On the other side of the hill?"

name: Tomosane
message: "There's a field of sunflowers on the hill. It would be pretty hard for Hasaki to get out there on foot."

name: Tomosane
message: "But Hasaki was always asking what was out there on the other side of the sunflowers."

name: Tomosane
message: "She liked to imagine that there was something out there."

name: Yuki
message: "Out there? It's just the mountain wilderness, isn't it?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah, that's why she might be in trouble. It's getting late."

name: Yuki
message: "Tomosane, we need to hurry!"

name: Tomosane
message: "Hasaki!"

name: Yuki
message: "Hasaki-chan!"
I searched the field of sunflowers with Yuki-nee.

name: Yuki
message: "These sunflowers are as tall as us. I can hardly see a thing."

name: Tomosane
message: "Maybe she kept going down this road."

name: Yuki
message: "Gotcha, I'll search down the road, so you just wait here."

name: Tomosane
message: "Why?"

name: Yuki
message: "You dummy, I can't let a child go wandering out in the mountains at night!"

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm not a kid!"

name: Yuki
message: "Hush! Just wait here!"

name: Tomosane
message: "Hey, Yuki-nee!"
Just like that, she ran off down the road.

name: Tomosane
message: "Damn it..."
To be honest, I can't run nearly as fast as her. Even if I ran after her, I couldn't catch her.
I had nothing to do but look around the sunflower field.

name: Tomosane
message: "Hasaki!"
The sunflowers were even taller than I was, and my voice was drowned out in the thick vegetation.
No matter how much I yelled, my words were simply swallowed up by the sunflowers.

name: Tomosane
message: "Damn it. She probably wouldn't hear me if she was here."
I kept yelling out into the field of sunflowers, but I wasn't accomplishing anything. Eventually, I went to search the top of the hill instead.
I figured my voice would carry further if I went up to the peak.
I stood up on a high place where I could look over the entire field, and as I was about to call out Hasaki's name again...

name: Tomosane
message: "Huh?"
Once I could see out over the entire field of sunflowers, I noticed something for the first time.

name: Tomosane
message: "Is that... a road?"
Over on the far side of the field, there was some kind of trail.
The trail led up to the main mountain road.

name: Tomosane
message: "There's another path over there. The sunflowers were so tall that I didn't even notice."
Yuki-nee told me to wait, but I couldn't keep that promise.
There was another road that Yuki-nee didn't go down.
It was possible that Hasaki actually went down this road.
So I...
I cut through the field of sunflowers and ran up the steep road.

name: Tomosane
message: "Haa, haa, haa, haa..."
I had no idea where the road would lead.
On the other side of the monstrous blooming sunflowers, that road led into the darkness itself, like something from beyond the pale of our own familiar world.
If Hasaki took my words literally...
Dad's soul is far away, in the sky. He made his journey to a place called Heaven.
If she took those words literally, she would want to follow this path to its end.
A path through the proud sunflowers into the heart of nature's darkness, like a path to connect the realms of life and death.
If there was any place that Hasaki would imagine her father's soul taking up its final residence, it would be this place, beyond the hill of sunflowers.
That's why I ran.
I ran up the steep hill with all my might.
I ran headlong into the night, and I saw a light before me.
It was like we were coming out upon the world's exterior.
A world of deep blue.
Brilliant, shining stars were woven into the tapestry.
I'd never seen such a night sky.
It looked like the sky was falling.

name: Tomosane
message: "What's this?"
I was taken aback by the sheer number of stars.
The starry night sky was always a beautiful sight in this village.
But in the three years I'd lived here, I had never seen such a beautiful sky. It was like...

name: Hasaki
message: "Like the souls of the dead flying away."

name: Tomosane
message: "Hasaki!"

name: Hasaki
message: "Tomo Nii-san!"

name: Tomosane
message: "Ah!"
I barely caught Hasaki before she fell.
She was totally covered in mud.
She probably stumbled and fell when she was climbing up here.
She was obviously so tired she could hardly stand.

name: Hasaki
message: "One of these... One of them is Dad's soul, right?"

name: Hasaki
message: "But I'm not tall enough... I can't reach him..."

name: Tomosane
message: "You dummy... It doesn't matter how tall you are, no one could reach Dad's soul just by reaching up."

name: Hasaki
message: "But they're all so close..."

name: Tomosane
message: "It doesn't matter. You can't reach Dad anymore."

name: Hasaki
message: "But we came all the way to the edge of the world..."
A sky full of stars that seemed about to rain down upon our heads.
I wasn't really surprised that Hasaki would think it was the edge of the world.
That this could've been the edge of the world.
Hasaki started to cry.

name: Hasaki
message: "This isn't the edge of the world... Tomo Nii-san!"

name: Hasaki
message: "Dad's soul isn't here..."
Hasaki cried into my chest.
I just held her and tried to comfort her.

name: Hasaki
message: "There's no edge to the world, is there? Look..."
Hasaki pointed with her finger.
But she didn't point toward Sawaimura.
It was another village, just like Sawaimura, lit up with the lights of human life.
There was another village out there. And after that, a bigger town. No matter how far she walked, she would always find something else out there.
No matter how far she tried to journey into the unknown, she could never reach the place where our father's soul had come to rest.
That was the first time Hasaki was forced to face that truth.
The truth of her father's death.
The true meaning of the word.
I held Hasaki close.

name: Tomosane
message: "Let's go back. Everyone is worried about you."

name: Hasaki
message: "Everyone is worried?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah, they really are. Everyone in the village is probably looking for you by now. You just disappeared on us, you know?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Looking for me?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah. If Dad knew you went out alone at night like this, he would have to scold you."

name: Hasaki
message: "I'm sorry..."

name: Tomosane
message: "You dummy. Just don't do it again."

name: Tomosane
message: "Come on."
I squatted down and gave Hasaki my back.

name: Tomosane
message: "I'll carry you. You can't walk, can you?"

name: Hasaki
message: "I-I'm not sure..."

name: Tomosane
message: "I just watched you fall a second ago. Hurry up and get on already."

name: Hasaki
message: "O-Okay..."

name: Hasaki
message: "I'm sorry..."

name: Tomosane
message: "What for?"

name: Hasaki
message: "For making you worry."

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah, no kidding."

name: Hasaki
message: "But how did you know where I was?"

name: Tomosane
message: "How? Are you stupid?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Huh? Why?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I told you, I'm your hero. What kind of hero would I be if I didn't come rescue you when you were in trouble?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Tomo Nii-san..."

name: Tomosane
message: "Heroes have to know things like that."

name: Hasaki
message: "I guess so."

name: Tomosane
message: "So of course I knew where you were."

name: Hasaki
message: "Um, Tomo Nii-san... Here."

name: Tomosane
message: "What? Isn't this your rabbit doll?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah, I'll give you one."

name: Tomosane
message: "Really? Won't the other one get lonely?"

name: Hasaki
message: "She has me, so she'll be okay."

name: Tomosane
message: "Ahaha... That's silly."

name: Tomosane
message: "Oh, right. In that case, there was that rabbit in the store window, right? I'll buy that for you."

name: Hasaki
message: "Huh? The store?"

name: Tomosane
message: "You know, the one you saw when we went to Jasco. The great big rabbit in the window."

name: Hasaki
message: "The stuffed rabbit from that store called Wakatsuki?"

name: Tomosane
message: "You still remember the store name too?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah, that rabbit was at the store called Wakatsuki."

name: Tomosane
message: "Oh, really? In that case, I'll buy the rabbit from that store for you."

name: Hasaki
message: "But it was expensive."

name: Tomosane
message: "Are you dumb? Don't underestimate me. My bank account is worth more than most countries' entire GDP."

name: Hasaki
message: "Really?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah, really. So don't worry about it."

name: Hasaki
message: "If that's true, then I'll give you this one too."

name: Tomosane
message: "Huh? Both of 'em?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah, that way neither of them will get lonely."

name: Tomosane
message: "You think?"

name: Hasaki
message: "When you give me the big rabbit, I'll be her twin."

name: Hasaki
message: "Then you can take both of the little rabbit twins."

name: Tomosane
message: "Are you sure?"
Then Hasaki went quiet.
I realized she'd fallen asleep.
She was obviously tired.

name: Tomosane
message: "But still..."
I told her I was her hero, but the truth is, it was just dumb luck that I found her.
Or maybe it really was Dad leading me to her.
They say a person's soul can stay on Earth for a while before leaving for Heaven.

name: Tomosane
message: "In that case, Dad, I hope you're listening."

name: Tomosane
message: "I... I'm gonna hold a grudge against you, Dad."

name: Tomosane
message: "Because you were always too nice to people. That's why this happened."

name: Tomosane
message: "So now that you're dead, just look after Hasaki."

name: Tomosane
message: "You just need to protect Hasaki."

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm not asking anything else."

name: Tomosane
message: "Just watch over her, so she can be happy."

name: Tomosane
message: "Please, Dad."
A starry sky, so full of stars that they could rain down upon our heads at any moment, laid above us.
I stood under just such a starry sky as I uttered the last words I would ever say to my father.
